curriculum.subject: Physics
curriculum.gradeLevel: Class XI / Class XII / 12th Grade / Science
curriculum.overallDescription: 19 units, 28 chapters, and 8 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Apply knowledge of fundamental and derived SI units for physical quantities.
Determine the number of significant figures in measured values and calculate results with appropriate precision.
Estimate and determine the uncertainty in experimental results using standard rules.
Derive dimensions of physical quantities and apply dimensional analysis to verify equations or derive relationships.
Use elementary concepts of differentiation and integration to describe kinematic quantities like velocity and acceleration.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity in various motion scenarios.
Interpret position-time and velocity-time graphs to analyze uniform and non-uniform accelerated motion.
Apply graphical methods and calculus-based relations for uniformly accelerated motion.
Perform addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, and dot/cross products on vectors; resolve vectors into rectangular components.
Analyze projectile motion by resolving initial velocity and applying kinematic equations in two dimensions.
Describe uniform circular motion including the relationship between linear speed, angular speed, and centripetal acceleration.
Explain the concept of inertia and apply Newton's first law to identify equilibrium conditions.

practicals.title: To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body and to measure internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using Vernier Callipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of a given wire and thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine volume of an irregular lamina using screw gauge.
To determine radius of curvature of a given spherical surface by a spherometer.
To determine the mass of two different objects using a beam balance.
To find the weight of a given body using parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum, plot its graph and use it to find the effective length of second's pendulum.
To study variation of time period of a simple pendulum of a given length by taking bobs of same size but different masses and interpret the result.
To study the relationship between force of limiting friction and normal reaction and to find the co- efficient of friction between a block and a horizontal surface.

To find the downward force, along an inclined plane, acting on a roller due to gravitational pull of the earth and study its relationship with the angle of inclination θ by plotting graph between force and Sinθ.
To determine Young's modulus of elasticity of the material of a given wire.
To find the force constant of a helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
To study the variation in volume with pressure for a sample of air at constant temperature by plotting graphs between P and V, and between P and 1/V.
practicals.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

practicals.title: To determine the surface tension of water by capillary rise method.
To determine the coefficient of viscosity of a given viscous liquid by measuring terminal velocity of a given spherical body.
To study the relationship between the temperature of a hot body and time by plotting a cooling curve.
To determine specific heat capacity of a given solid by method of mixtures.
To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using sonometer.
To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension for constant frequency using sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air at room temperature using a resonance tube by two resonance positions.
To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current.
To find resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using metre bridge.
To verify the laws of combination (series) of resistances using a metre bridge OR To verify the laws of combination (parallel) of resistances using a metre bridge.
To determine resistance of a galvanometer by half-deflection method and to find its figure of merit.

To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into a voltmeter of desired range and to verify the same OR To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into an ammeter of desired range and to verify the same.
To find the frequency of AC mains with a sonometer.
To find the value of v for different values of u in case of a concave mirror and to find the focal length.
To find the focal length of a convex mirror, using a convex lens.
To find the focal length of a convex lens by plotting graphs between u and v or between 1/u and 1/v.
To find the focal length of a concave lens, using a convex lens.
To determine angle of minimum deviation for a given prism by plotting a graph between angle of incidence and angle of deviation.
To determine refractive index of a glass slab using a travelling microscope.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using convex lens and plane mirror.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using a concave mirror and a plane mirror.
To draw the I-V characteristic curve for a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias.
To determine the resistance per cm of a given wire by plotting a graph between voltage and current.
To verify the laws of combination (series/parallel combination) of resistances by Ohm's law.
To find the resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using a meter bridge.
To determine the resistance of a galvanometer by half deflection method.
To identify a resistor, capacitor, inductor and diode from a mixed collection of such items.

To observe the difference between (i) a convex lens and a concave lens (ii) a convex mirror and a concave mirror and to estimate the likely difference between the power of two given convex /concave lenses.
To design an inductor coil and to know the effect of (i) change in the number of turns (ii) Introduction of ferromagnetic material as its core material on the inductance of the coil.
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

practicals.title: To design a (i) step up (ii) step down transformer on a given core and know the relation between its input and output voltages.
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

activities.title: To make a paper scale of given least count, e.g., 0.2cm, 0.5 cm.
To determine mass of a given body using a metre scale by principle of moments.
To plot a graph for a given set of data, with proper choice of scales and error bars.
To measure the force of limiting friction for rolling of a roller on a horizontal plane.
To study the variation in range of a projectile with angle of projection.
To study the conservation of energy of a ball rolling down on an inclined plane (using a double inclined plane).
To study dissipation of energy of a simple pendulum by plotting a graph between square of amplitude and time.
To observe change of state and plot a cooling curve for molten wax.
To observe and explain the effect of heating on a bi-metallic strip.
To note the change in level of liquid in a container on heating and interpret the observations.
To study the effect of detergent on surface tension of water by observing capillary rise.
To study the factors affecting the rate of loss of heat of a liquid.
To study the effect of load on depression of a suitably clamped metre scale loaded at (i) its end (ii) in the middle.
To observe the decrease in pressure with increase in velocity of a fluid.
To measure the resistance and impedance of an inductor with or without iron core.
To measure resistance, voltage (AC/DC), current (AC) and check continuity of a given circuit using multimeter.
To assemble a household circuit comprising three bulbs, three (on/off) switches, a fuse and a power source.
To assemble the components of a given electrical circuit.
To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current.

To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit and also the circuit diagram.
To identify a diode, an LED, a resistor and a capacitor from a mixed collection of such items.
Use of multimeter to see the unidirectional flow of current in case of a diode and an LED and check whether a given electronic component (e.g., diode) is in working order.
To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR.
To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab.
To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.
To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen (for different distances of the candle from the lens/mirror).
To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses.

projects.title: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.

To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of a variation in (a) the power of the incandescent lamp, used to 'illuminate' the LDR (keeping all the lamps at a fixed distance). (b) the distance of an incandescent lamp (of fixed power) used to 'illuminate' the LDR.
To find the refractive indices of (a) water (b) oil (transparent) using a plane mirror, an equiconvex lens (made from a glass of known refractive index) and an adjustable object needle.
To investigate the relation between the ratio of (i) output and input voltage and (ii) number of turns in the secondary coil and primary coil of a self-designed transformer.
projects.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

projects.title: To investigate the dependence of the angle of deviation on the angle of incidence using a hollow prism filled one by one, with different transparent fluids.
To estimate the charge induced on each one of the two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane by making use of Coulomb's law.

To study the factor on which the self-inductance of a coil depends by observing the effect of this coil, when put in series with a resistor/(bulb) in a circuit fed up by an A.C. source of adjustable frequency.
projects.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

projects.title: To study the earth's magnetic field using a compass needle -bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer.

marks_distribution: Class XI/XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 hours
Class XI/XII Practical: Max Marks 30, Time 2-3 hours (Visually Impaired: 2 hours)

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts; Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way.

bloom_levels.note: Included in Remembering/Analysing grouping for marks calculation

internal_assessment: One Activity from any section: 3 Marks (Class XI/XII)
Viva on experiments, activities and project: 5 Marks (Class XI/XII)

practical_assessment.description: One from each section (A & B) for Class XI; One from each section (A & B) for Class XII.
Record of at least 8 Experiments and 6 Activities (Class XI/XII). For Visually Impaired: At least five experiments recorded.
From any section (Class XI/XII). For Visually Impaired: Not explicitly listed as separate marks in VI scheme but part of record.
Identification/Familiarity (5), Written Test (10 marks/30 mins duration, 15 questions answer any 10)

project_assessment.description: Report of the project carried out by students.

suggested_projects_class_xi: Study various factors on which internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends
Variations in current flowing in circuit containing LDR due to variation in power/distance of lamp
Find refractive indices of water/oil using plane mirror and lens
Investigate relation between output/input voltage and turns ratio in transformer
Dependence of angle of deviation on angle of incidence using hollow prism
Estimate charge induced on Styrofoam balls using Coulomb's law
Study factor on which self-inductance depends
Study earth's magnetic field using compass needle/bar magnet
suggested_projects_class_xii: Same as Class XI list (shared suggested projects)

practicals.title: To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body and to measure internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using Vernier Callipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of a given wire and thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine volume of an irregular lamina using screw gauge.
To determine radius of curvature of a given spherical surface by a spherometer.
To determine the mass of two different objects using a beam balance.
To find the weight of a given body using parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum, plot its graph and use it to find the effective length of second's pendulum.
To study variation of time period of a simple pendulum of a given length by taking bobs of same size but different masses and interpret the result.
To study the relationship between force of limiting friction and normal reaction and to find the co- efficient of friction between a block and a horizontal surface.

To find the downward force, along an inclined plane, acting on a roller due to gravitational pull of the earth and study its relationship with the angle of inclination θ by plotting graph between force and Sinθ.
To determine Young's modulus of elasticity of the material of a given wire.
To find the force constant of a helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
To study the variation in volume with pressure for a sample of air at constant temperature by plotting graphs between P and V, and between P and 1/V.
practicals.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

practicals.title: To determine the surface tension of water by capillary rise method.
To determine the coefficient of viscosity of a given viscous liquid by measuring terminal velocity of a given spherical body.
To study the relationship between the temperature of a hot body and time by plotting a cooling curve.
To determine specific heat capacity of a given solid by method of mixtures.
To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using sonometer.
To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension for constant frequency using sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air at room temperature using a resonance tube by two resonance positions.
To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current.
To find resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using metre bridge.
To verify the laws of combination (series) of resistances using a metre bridge OR To verify the laws of combination (parallel) of resistances using a metre bridge.
To determine resistance of a galvanometer by half-deflection method and to find its figure of merit.

To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into a voltmeter of desired range and to verify the same OR To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into an ammeter of desired range and to verify the same.
To find the frequency of AC mains with a sonometer.
To find the value of v for different values of u in case of a concave mirror and to find the focal length.
To find the focal length of a convex mirror, using a convex lens.
To find the focal length of a convex lens by plotting graphs between u and v or between 1/u and 1/v.
To find the focal length of a concave lens, using a convex lens.
To determine angle of minimum deviation for a given prism by plotting a graph between angle of incidence and angle of deviation.
To determine refractive index of a glass slab using a travelling microscope.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using convex lens and plane mirror.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using a concave mirror and a plane mirror.
To draw the I-V characteristic curve for a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias.
To determine the resistance per cm of a given wire by plotting a graph between voltage and current.
To verify the laws of combination (series/parallel combination) of resistances by Ohm's law.
To find the resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using a meter bridge.
To determine the resistance of a galvanometer by half deflection method.
To identify a resistor, capacitor, inductor and diode from a mixed collection of such items.

To observe the difference between (i) a convex lens and a concave lens (ii) a convex mirror and a concave mirror and to estimate the likely difference between the power of two given convex /concave lenses.
To design an inductor coil and to know the effect of (i) change in the number of turns (ii) Introduction of ferromagnetic material as its core material on the inductance of the coil.
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

practicals.title: To design a (i) step up (ii) step down transformer on a given core and know the relation between its input and output voltages.
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

activities.title: To make a paper scale of given least count, e.g., 0.2cm, 0.5 cm.
To determine mass of a given body using a metre scale by principle of moments.
To plot a graph for a given set of data, with proper choice of scales and error bars.
To measure the force of limiting friction for rolling of a roller on a horizontal plane.
To study the variation in range of a projectile with angle of projection.
To study the conservation of energy of a ball rolling down on an inclined plane (using a double inclined plane).
To study dissipation of energy of a simple pendulum by plotting a graph between square of amplitude and time.
To observe change of state and plot a cooling curve for molten wax.
To observe and explain the effect of heating on a bi-metallic strip.
To note the change in level of liquid in a container on heating and interpret the observations.
To study the effect of detergent on surface tension of water by observing capillary rise.
To study the factors affecting the rate of loss of heat of a liquid.
To study the effect of load on depression of a suitably clamped metre scale loaded at (i) its end (ii) in the middle.
To observe the decrease in pressure with increase in velocity of a fluid.
To measure the resistance and impedance of an inductor with or without iron core.
To measure resistance, voltage (AC/DC), current (AC) and check continuity of a given circuit using multimeter.
To assemble a household circuit comprising three bulbs, three (on/off) switches, a fuse and a power source.
To assemble the components of a given electrical circuit.
To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current.

To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit and also the circuit diagram.
To identify a diode, an LED, a resistor and a capacitor from a mixed collection of such items.
Use of multimeter to see the unidirectional flow of current in case of a diode and an LED and check whether a given electronic component (e.g., diode) is in working order.
To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR.
To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab.
To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.
To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen (for different distances of the candle from the lens/mirror).
To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses.

projects.title: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.

To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of a variation in (a) the power of the incandescent lamp, used to 'illuminate' the LDR (keeping all the lamps at a fixed distance). (b) the distance of an incandescent lamp (of fixed power) used to 'illuminate' the LDR.
To find the refractive indices of (a) water (b) oil (transparent) using a plane mirror, an equiconvex lens (made from a glass of known refractive index) and an adjustable object needle.
To investigate the relation between the ratio of (i) output and input voltage and (ii) number of turns in the secondary coil and primary coil of a self-designed transformer.
projects.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

projects.title: To investigate the dependence of the angle of deviation on the angle of incidence using a hollow prism filled one by one, with different transparent fluids.
To estimate the charge induced on each one of the two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane by making use of Coulomb's law.

To study the factor on which the self-inductance of a coil depends by observing the effect of this coil, when put in series with a resistor/(bulb) in a circuit fed up by an A.C. source of adjustable frequency.
projects.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

projects.title: To study the earth's magnetic field using a compass needle -bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer.

marks_distribution: Class XI/XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 hours
Class XI/XII Practical: Max Marks 30, Time 2-3 hours (Visually Impaired: 2 hours)

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts; Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way.

bloom_levels.note: Included in Remembering/Analysing grouping for marks calculation

internal_assessment: One Activity from any section: 3 Marks (Class XI/XII)
Viva on experiments, activities and project: 5 Marks (Class XI/XII)

practical_assessment.description: One from each section (A & B) for Class XI; One from each section (A & B) for Class XII.
Record of at least 8 Experiments and 6 Activities (Class XI/XII). For Visually Impaired: At least five experiments recorded.
From any section (Class XI/XII). For Visually Impaired: Not explicitly listed as separate marks in VI scheme but part of record.
Identification/Familiarity (5), Written Test (10 marks/30 mins duration, 15 questions answer any 10)

project_assessment.description: Report of the project carried out by students.

suggested_projects_class_xi: Study various factors on which internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends
Variations in current flowing in circuit containing LDR due to variation in power/distance of lamp
Find refractive indices of water/oil using plane mirror and lens
Investigate relation between output/input voltage and turns ratio in transformer
Dependence of angle of deviation on angle of incidence using hollow prism
Estimate charge induced on Styrofoam balls using Coulomb's law
Study factor on which self-inductance depends
Study earth's magnetic field using compass needle/bar magnet
suggested_projects_class_xii: Same as Class XI list (shared suggested projects)

competency_groups.title: Units and Measurements Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to measurement systems, SI units, significant figures, uncertainty determination, dimensions, and dimensional analysis.
competencies: Apply knowledge of fundamental and derived SI units for physical quantities.
Determine the number of significant figures in measured values and calculate results with appropriate precision.
Estimate and determine the uncertainty in experimental results using standard rules.
Derive dimensions of physical quantities and apply dimensional analysis to verify equations or derive relationships.

competency_groups.title: Motion in a Straight Line Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to describing motion using calculus, analyzing uniform/non-uniform motion, interpreting graphs, and applying equations of motion.
competencies: Use elementary concepts of differentiation and integration to describe kinematic quantities like velocity and acceleration.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity in various motion scenarios.
Interpret position-time and velocity-time graphs to analyze uniform and non-uniform accelerated motion.
Apply graphical methods and calculus-based relations for uniformly accelerated motion.

competency_groups.title: Motion in a Plane Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to vector operations, resolution of vectors, and analyzing two-dimensional motion including projectiles and circular paths.
competencies: Perform addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, and dot/cross products on vectors; resolve vectors into rectangular components.
Analyze projectile motion by resolving initial velocity and applying kinematic equations in two dimensions.
Describe uniform circular motion including the relationship between linear speed, angular speed, and centripetal acceleration.

competency_groups.title: Laws of Motion Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Newton's laws, momentum conservation, impulse, and forces including friction.
competencies: Explain the concept of inertia and apply Newton's first law to identify equilibrium conditions.
Apply Newton's second law (F=ma) and the concept of impulse to solve problems involving force and change in motion.
Utilize Newton's third law and the principle of conservation of linear momentum for systems interacting via forces.

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy and Power Competencies
competency_groups.description: Core competencies for Work, Energy and Power.
competencies: Understand the core concepts of Work, Energy and Power.
Apply key ideas from Work, Energy and Power to solve curriculum-aligned problems.
Analyze relationships between major concepts in Work, Energy and Power.

competency_groups.title: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to momentum conservation, torque, angular momentum, equilibrium, and rotational motion equations.
competencies: Apply the law of conservation of linear momentum to analyze centre of mass motion in a system of particles.
competency_groups.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to universal gravitation, variation of gravity, potential energy, orbital mechanics, and Kepler's laws.
competencies: Apply the Universal Law of Gravitation to calculate gravitational force between masses.

competency_groups.title: Mechanical Properties of Solids
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to elasticity, stress-strain relationships, and material properties like Young's modulus.
competencies: Apply Hooke's Law to determine the relationship between stress and strain in elastic materials.

competency_groups.title: Mechanical Properties of Fluids
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to fluid viscosity, flow dynamics, Bernoulli's theorem, and surface tension phenomena.
competencies: Apply Stokes' Law and the concept of terminal velocity to analyze motion through viscous fluids.

competency_groups.title: Thermal Properties of Matter
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to heat transfer, specific heats, latent heat, and blackbody radiation laws.
competencies: Apply the principles of calorimetry and conservation of energy to solve problems involving specific heat capacity.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from the Thermodynamics chapter.
competencies: Understand heat, work and internal energy concepts.
Apply the first law of thermodynamics to physical processes.
Analyze state variables and equations of state in relation to the second law.
Differentiate between isothermal and adiabatic changes in gaseous states.

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from the Kinetic Theory chapter.
competencies: Explain kinetic theory assumptions and derive the concept of pressure.
Interpret temperature through kinetic energy and calculate RMS speed.
Apply the law of equipartition of energy to specific heat capacities.
competency_groups.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Oscillations Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from the Oscillations chapter.
competencies: Describe periodic functions and their applications in displacement over time.
Analyze Simple Harmonic Motion (S.H.M) characteristics.

competency_groups.title: Waves Competencies
competency_groups.description: Core competencies for Waves.
competencies: Understand the core concepts of Waves.
Apply key ideas from Waves to solve curriculum-aligned problems.
Analyze relationships between major concepts in Waves.

competency_groups.title: Electric Charges and Fields Competencies
competency_groups.description: Mastering electrostatic principles including charge conservation, field calculations for various geometries, and Gauss's law applications.
competencies: Apply Coulomb's Law and Superposition Principle to calculate forces in continuous charge distributions.
Derive electric fields due to point charges, dipoles, wires, planes, and shells using symmetry arguments.
Utilize Gauss's theorem to determine flux and field intensity for symmetric charge configurations.

competency_groups.title: Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance Competencies
competency_groups.description: Understanding electric potential, energy storage in capacitors, and the role of dielectrics.
competencies: Calculate electrostatic potential and potential energy for systems of point charges and dipoles.
Analyze series and parallel combinations of capacitors to determine equivalent capacitance.
Explain the effect of dielectric materials on capacitor performance and polarization.

competency_groups.title: Current Electricity Competencies
competency_groups.description: Analyzing current flow, resistance characteristics, and complex circuit analysis using Kirchhoff's laws.
competencies: Relate drift velocity to electric field and explain temperature dependence of resistivity.
Distinguish between EMF and potential difference in circuits with internal resistance.
Apply Kirchhoff's rules and Wheatstone bridge principles to solve complex circuit problems.

competency_groups.title: Moving Charges and Magnetism Competencies
competency_groups.description: Exploring magnetic field generation, forces on moving charges, and the operation of galvanometers.
competencies: Apply Biot-Savart law and Ampere's circuital law to calculate magnetic fields from various current sources.
Analyze force and torque experienced by charged particles and current loops in uniform magnetic fields.
Convert a moving coil galvanometer into an ammeter or voltmeter based on sensitivity requirements.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Magnetism and Matter Competencies
competency_groups.description: Understanding the magnetic properties of materials and their equivalence to current loops.
competencies: Describe bar magnets as equivalent solenoids based on qualitative field patterns.
Identify fundamental properties distinguishing diamagnetic, paramagnetic, and ferromagnetic substances.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction Competencies
competency_groups.description: Core competencies for understanding electromagnetic induction principles and applications.
competencies: Apply Faraday's Law to calculate induced emf in varying magnetic fields.
Analyze Lenz's Law to determine the direction of induced current.
Differentiate between motional and transformer-induced electromotive force.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Alternating Current Competencies
competency_groups.description: Core competencies for analyzing alternating current circuits and components.
competencies: Calculate peak, RMS values, and phase relationships in AC circuits.
Analyze the behavior of resistors, capacitors, and inductors in series RLC circuits.
Determine resonance conditions and quality factor in LC oscillations.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves Competencies
competency_groups.description: Core competencies for understanding the nature and propagation of electromagnetic waves.
competencies: Describe the transverse nature and spectrum of electromagnetic waves.
Explain Maxwell's equations regarding displacement current and wave generation.
Relate energy density, intensity, and momentum transfer in EM radiation.

competency_groups.title: Ray Optics Competencies
competency_groups.description: Core competencies for geometric optics, reflection, refraction, and optical instruments.
competencies: Apply laws of reflection and refraction to solve image formation problems.
Derive lens maker's formula and analyze magnification in lenses and mirrors.
Explain the working principles of microscopes, telescopes, and prisms.

competency_groups.title: Wave Optics Competencies
competency_groups.description: Core competencies for wave nature of light, interference, diffraction, and polarization.
competencies: Apply Huygens' principle to explain reflection, refraction, and propagation.
Analyze Young's double-slit experiment conditions for constructive and destructive interference.
Differentiate between phenomena of diffraction and interference in light waves.

competency_groups.title: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.description: Understanding the particle nature of light, photoelectric effect, wave-particle duality, de Broglie hypothesis, electron diffraction, Davisson-Germer experiment, Compton scattering, X-ray production, and applications in modern physics.
competencies: Explain experimental evidence for the dual nature of radiation (photoelectric effect) and matter (electron diffraction).
Derive and apply Einstein's photoelectric equation to calculate stopping potential and kinetic energy of emitted electrons.
Analyze Compton scattering phenomena and explain wave-particle duality using de Broglie wavelength concepts.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.chapter_name: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

competency_groups.title: Atoms
competency_groups.description: Exploring atomic structure through Rutherford's scattering experiment, Bohr's model postulates, hydrogen spectral series (Lyman, Balmer, Paschen), energy levels, and limitations of the classical models.
competencies: Describe Rutherford's alpha-particle scattering experiment and deduce the nuclear model of the atom.
Apply Bohr's quantization conditions to calculate orbital radii, velocities, and energies for hydrogen-like atoms.
Explain the origin of atomic spectra using energy level transitions and derive wavelengths for various spectral series.

competency_groups.title: Nuclei
competency_groups.description: Investigating nuclear composition, mass defect, binding energy per nucleon curve, radioactive decay laws (alpha, beta, gamma), half-life calculations, and applications in fission and fusion.
competencies: Calculate mass defect and binding energy to explain nuclear stability using the binding energy per nucleon graph.
Apply the law of radioactive decay to determine activity, mean life, and half-life for given isotopes.
Differentiate between nuclear fission and fusion processes and analyze their energy release mechanisms.

competency_groups.title: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
competency_groups.description: Studying semiconductor physics including energy bands, intrinsic vs extrinsic semiconductors (n-type/p-type), p-n junction diode characteristics, rectifiers, Zener diodes as voltage regulators, transistors in common emitter configuration, logic gates, and basic circuit applications.
competencies: Distinguish between conductors, insulators, and semiconductors based on energy band theory and doping effects.
Analyze the V-I characteristics of p-n junction diodes to explain rectification processes in half-wave and full-wave circuits.
Design simple logic gate combinations using NAND/NOR gates and analyze transistor operation as a switch or amplifier.

activities.title: To make a paper scale of given least count, e.g., 0.2cm, 0.5 cm.
To determine mass of a given body using a metre scale by principle of moments.
To plot a graph for a given set of data, with proper choice of scales and error bars.
To measure the force of limiting friction for rolling of a roller on a horizontal plane.
To study the variation in range of a projectile with angle of projection.
To study the conservation of energy of a ball rolling down on an inclined plane (using a double inclined plane).
To study dissipation of energy of a simple pendulum by plotting a graph between square of amplitude and time.
To observe change of state and plot a cooling curve for molten wax.
To observe and explain the effect of heating on a bi-metallic strip.
To note the change in level of liquid in a container on heating and interpret the observations.
To study the effect of detergent on surface tension of water by observing capillary rise.
To study the factors affecting the rate of loss of heat of a liquid.
To study the effect of load on depression of a suitably clamped metre scale loaded at (i) its end (ii) in the middle.
To observe the decrease in pressure with increase in velocity of a fluid.
To measure the resistance and impedance of an inductor with or without iron core.
To measure resistance, voltage (AC/DC), current (AC) and check continuity of a given circuit using multimeter.
To assemble a household circuit comprising three bulbs, three (on/off) switches, a fuse and a power source.
To assemble the components of a given electrical circuit.
To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current.

To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit and also the circuit diagram.
To identify a diode, an LED, a resistor and a capacitor from a mixed collection of such items.
Use of multimeter to see the unidirectional flow of current in case of a diode and an LED and check whether a given electronic component (e.g., diode) is in working order.
To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR.
To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab.
To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.
To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen (for different distances of the candle from the lens/mirror).
To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses.

projects.title: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.

To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of a variation in (a) the power of the incandescent lamp, used to 'illuminate' the LDR (keeping all the lamps at a fixed distance). (b) the distance of an incandescent lamp (of fixed power) used to 'illuminate' the LDR.
To find the refractive indices of (a) water (b) oil (transparent) using a plane mirror, an equiconvex lens (made from a glass of known refractive index) and an adjustable object needle.
To investigate the relation between the ratio of (i) output and input voltage and (ii) number of turns in the secondary coil and primary coil of a self-designed transformer.
projects.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

projects.title: To investigate the dependence of the angle of deviation on the angle of incidence using a hollow prism filled one by one, with different transparent fluids.
To estimate the charge induced on each one of the two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane by making use of Coulomb's law.

To study the factor on which the self-inductance of a coil depends by observing the effect of this coil, when put in series with a resistor/(bulb) in a circuit fed up by an A.C. source of adjustable frequency.

projects.title: To study the earth's magnetic field using a compass needle -bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer.

To measure diameter of a given wire and thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine volume of an irregular lamina using screw gauge.
To determine radius of curvature of a given spherical surface by a spherometer.
To determine the mass of two different objects using a beam balance.
To find the weight of a given body using parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum, plot its graph and use it to find the effective length of second's pendulum.
To study variation of time period of a simple pendulum of a given length by taking bobs of same size but different masses and interpret the result.
To study the relationship between force of limiting friction and normal reaction and to find the co- efficient of friction between a block and a horizontal surface.

To find the downward force, along an inclined plane, acting on a roller due to gravitational pull of the earth and study its relationship with the angle of inclination θ by plotting graph between force and Sinθ.
To determine Young's modulus of elasticity of the material of a given wire.
To find the force constant of a helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
To study the variation in volume with pressure for a sample of air at constant temperature by plotting graphs between P and V, and between P and 1/V.

practicals.title: To determine the surface tension of water by capillary rise method.
To determine the coefficient of viscosity of a given viscous liquid by measuring terminal velocity of a given spherical body.
To study the relationship between the temperature of a hot body and time by plotting a cooling curve.
To determine specific heat capacity of a given solid by method of mixtures.
To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using sonometer.
To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension for constant frequency using sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air at room temperature using a resonance tube by two resonance positions.
To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current.
To find resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using metre bridge.
To verify the laws of combination (series) of resistances using a metre bridge OR To verify the laws of combination (parallel) of resistances using a metre bridge.
To determine resistance of a galvanometer by half-deflection method and to find its figure of merit.

To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into a voltmeter of desired range and to verify the same OR To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into an ammeter of desired range and to verify the same.
To find the frequency of AC mains with a sonometer.
To find the value of v for different values of u in case of a concave mirror and to find the focal length.
To find the focal length of a convex mirror, using a convex lens.
To find the focal length of a convex lens by plotting graphs between u and v or between 1/u and 1/v.
To find the focal length of a concave lens, using a convex lens.
To determine angle of minimum deviation for a given prism by plotting a graph between angle of incidence and angle of deviation.
To determine refractive index of a glass slab using a travelling microscope.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using convex lens and plane mirror.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using a concave mirror and a plane mirror.
To draw the I-V characteristic curve for a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias.
To determine the resistance per cm of a given wire by plotting a graph between voltage and current.

To determine the resistance of a galvanometer by half deflection method.
To identify a resistor, capacitor, inductor and diode from a mixed collection of such items.
